mappings.rationaleAI: Covers Mesopotamian civilizations 3500-539 BCE including Sumerian cuneiform and city-states (B.1.b.i, B.1.b.ii), Akkadian ascendancy (B.1.c.i), Ur III (B.1.c.ii), early Assyria and Hammurabi (B.1.d.ii, B.1.d.iii), Hurrian expansion (B.1.e), Hittite Anatolia (D.2.a.iv), Hittite Empire (E.2), Kassites (E.3), Assyria after 900 BC (F.1.c), Aramaean role (F.2.f), Assyrian Empire (G.1), and Neo-Babylonian Empire (G.2.a).
Covers pharaonic civilization from earliest dynasties (C.1), Old Kingdom pyramids (C.2.a), Middle Kingdom (C.3), New Kingdom (E.1), and post-New Kingdom decline (F.1.a).
Egyptian arts connect to Egyptian civilization (C) and New Kingdom culture (E.1.c).
Egyptian mythology connects to Egyptian civilization (C), Amon-Re cult (E.1.a.i), and New Kingdom culture (E.1.c).
Hieroglyphs connect to sources (A), Early Dynastic (C.1), Old Kingdom (C.2), and New Kingdom (E.1.c).
Covers Hammurabi (B.1.d.iii), Kassites (E.3.a), Babylonia 1050-750 BC (F.1.b), Assyrian period (G.1), Nebuchadnezzar and Jewish Exile (G.2.a.i), and last Babylonian kings (G.2.a.ii).
Covers early Assyria (B.1.d.ii), rise (E.3.c), dominance (F.1.c), culture (G.1.a), and expansion through decline (G.1.b).
Trojan War connects to Bronze Age Troy (D.2.a.iii) and Mycenaean civilization (E.5).
Cuneiform and hieroglyphs connect to sources (A), Sumerian writing (B.1.b.i), and Canaanite alphabet (E.2.b.i).
Biblical archaeology covers Syria-Palestine (D.1), Hebrew Exodus (E.2.b.ii), Hebrew kingdom (F.2.b, F.2.b.i), and Babylonian Exile (G.2.a.i).
David stories connect to Hebrew kingdom (F.2.b) and Yahweh cult (F.2.b.ii).
Judaism connects to Hebrew kingdom and Yahweh (F.2.b, F.2.b.ii) and the Exile (G.2.a.i).
Iran connects to early Elam (B.2), Achaemenid establishment (G.2.d), Zoroastrianism (G.3.c), and Parthian-Sasanian empires (H).
Alexander's conquest addresses Xerxes' successors and conquest (G.3.b).
Hellenistic world covers Seleucid rule (G.3.d).
Ancient warfare covers Assyrian expansion (G.1.b) and Persian attempts on Greece (G.3.a).
Trade routes connect to Parthian and Sasanian empires (H).
Archaeological methods connect to sources (A), Neolithic periods (B.1.a), and Stone Age cultures (D.1.a).
African history covers Nilotic Sudan (I.1), Kingdom of Kush (I.2), and North Africa (I.5).
Late antiquity connects to Sasanian decline and Arab conquest (H.2.c).
Old Testament covers Hebrew kingdom (F.2.b, F.2.b.ii) and Babylonian Exile (G.2.a.i).
Dead Sea Scrolls connect to Yahweh cult (F.2.b.ii).
Indus Valley civilization provides Near Eastern cultural context (D.1).
Ancient Greece covers Aegean civilizations (D.2.c), Mycenaean period (E.5), and Persian wars (G.3.a).
The Bible connects to Yahweh cult (F.2.b.ii).
Islamic history connects to Sasanian extinction through Arab conquest (H.2.c).
Prehistory covers sources (A), Neolithic periods (B.1.a), and Paleolithic development (D.1.a.i).
